claim_text: The evaluation finds no single agent framework that leads across all three code-centric tasks, because different frameworks post the top reported results in software development, vulnerability detection, and program repair.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the results show clear cross-task tension because the strongest framework changes by task.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence supports a cross-task comparison of outcomes only; it does not establish the causal mechanism for why leadership changes by task.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the results show clear cross-task tension because the strongest framework changes by task.

claim_text: In the software-development benchmark, OpenHands leads the overall quality score at 0.47, but the strongest submetrics are split across frameworks rather than concentrated in one system.
caveats: Visible tension is present: OpenHands leads overall quality and executability, AgentOrchestra leads completeness, and GPTswarm leads consistency.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence shows metric splitting across quality, completeness, executability, and consistency, but it does not justify a causal account of why the metrics separate across frameworks.
contradiction_note: Visible tension is present: OpenHands leads overall quality and executability, AgentOrchestra leads completeness, and GPTswarm leads consistency.

claim_text: GPTswarm posts the strongest reported vulnerability-detection result at 80 of 115 cases, but Gas Limitation vulnerabilities remain rarely detected across the evaluated agents.
caveats: Visible tension is present: the top overall vulnerability detector still does not remove a category-level weakness shared across agents.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence supports a performance bound that success in overall detection does not extend reliably to Gas Limitation cases; the mechanism for that weakness is not established here.
contradiction_note: Visible tension is present: the top overall vulnerability detector still does not remove a category-level weakness shared across agents.

claim_text: In program repair, patch-tooling capability appears to matter materially, because frameworks reported as lacking patch tooling and correct diff-format patch generation perform poorly while SE-Agent (Iter-3) leads with 161 of 300 repairs.
caveats: Visible tension remains: even with a leading repair result, no evaluated agent repairs any Flask issue in the benchmark.
mechanism_or_bounds: The supported mechanism is that absent patch tooling and failure to generate correct diff-format patches constrain repair effectiveness; this is bounded to the reported frameworks and benchmarks and does not explain all failures, including the universal failure on the three Flask issues.
contradiction_note: Visible tension remains: even with a leading repair result, no evaluated agent repairs any Flask issue in the benchmark.
